sections.paragraph:
m. (from the root שָׁכַז pl. ־ִים Psa 46:5 more often in ־ֹות construed with a fem. Psa 84:2)

(1) habitation, dwelling place, as of men, Job 18:21 Psa 87:2. (once used of the long home, the sepulchre, Isa 22:16 compare 14:18 , and my note on the passage); of animals, i.e. den, Job 39:6 of God, i.e. temple, Psa 46:5 84:2 132:5 .

(2) specially a tent, a tabernacle, Son 1:8, especially the holy tabernacle of the Israelites, Exo 25:9 26:1 , seq.; 40:9 , seq.; fully מִשְׁכַּן הָעֵדוּת “the tabernacle of the testimony,” Exo 38:21 Num 1:50, 53 10:11 . In the description of this tent, as to the difference of מִשְׁכָּן from אֹהֶל see above at that word, p. 17, A hence מִשְׁכַּז אֹהֶל מֹועֵד of the boards of the holy tent, over which the curtains were hung, Exo 39:32 40:2 , Exo 40:6, 29 Exo 40:29.
